representations.name: AspNetUsers
representations.rationale: Source of truth for all authentication. Stores password hash, roles, lockout state. Managed by UserManager<ApplicationUser>.

representations.name: Baskets.BuyerId
representations.rationale: Stores AspNetUsers.UserName (email) as a plain string. No FK to IdentityDB. Also stores anonymous GUID values for guest users.

representations.name: Orders.BuyerId
representations.rationale: Stores email at time of order. Historical record — intentionally not FK-linked to IdentityDB.

representations.name: Buyer (BuyerAggregate)
representations.rationale: Defines IdentityGuid + PaymentMethods. NOT in CatalogContext DbSets. NOT in any migration. Never constructed or queried anywhere in codebase or test files.
representations.finding: DEAD CODE — entity exists in domain layer but has zero persistence and zero active use.

canonical_shadow_checks.redundancy_risk: MEDIUM — BuyerId duplicated as string across Baskets and Orders with no FK. Right-to-erasure requires cleanup in 3+ places across 2 databases.

representations.name: CatalogItem (entity) → Catalog (table)
representations.rationale: Master record. Updated by admin. Queried by storefront. Source of truth for current product details.

representations.name: CatalogItemOrdered (value object) → OrderItems.ItemOrdered_* columns
representations.rationale: Snapshot at purchase time. Explicitly documented in XML doc: 'If catalog item details change, details of the item that was part of a completed order should not change.'

representations.name: BlazorShared.Models.CatalogItem
representations.rationale: DTO for Blazor/API communication. Adds UI concerns (PictureBase64, PictureName) absent from the domain entity.

canonical_shadow_checks.redundancy_risk: LOW — DTOs are legitimate view models; snapshot is intentional by design.

representations.name: PaymentMethod (BuyerAggregate)
representations.rationale: Defines CardId, Last4, Alias. NOT mapped to any EF context. NOT in any migration. Never instantiated in any service, repository, handler, or test file. Comment: 'actual card data must be stored in a PCI compliant system, like Stripe'.
representations.finding: DEAD CODE — placeholder entity, not persisted, not used.

canonical_shadow_checks.canonical_representation: NONE — no canonical persistence exists for payment methods in this codebase

agent2_review_changes.what: Dead code status confirmed by test evidence. No test file in any of the four test suites (UnitTests, IntegrationTests, FunctionalTests, PublicApiIntegrationTests) references Buyer or PaymentMethod entities. 49 test files scanned — zero hits.
agent2_review_changes.evidence_detail: Glob of all 49 test .cs files shows no import of BuyerAggregate namespace. No builder/factory for Buyer or PaymentMethod in tests/UnitTests/Builders/.

intentional_duplicates: CatalogItemOrdered — order snapshot of CatalogItem, correct by design and explicitly documented
denormalized_references: Baskets.BuyerId — email stored as string, no FK to IdentityDB
Orders.BuyerId — email stored as string, no FK to IdentityDB
